story: After the garage blast, Karen dragged Peter from smoke; neighbors pulled them clear. In hospital, Robert wheeled her toward a curtained bed marked "Peter" where a fully bandaged patient lay under wires and gauze. In a wheelchair near the foot of that bed sat the real Peter—singed and alert, ready to speak. Monitors ticked; the curtain rustled. Karen, shaky and tearful, reached the bedside rail.
implausible_knowledge: In a wheelchair near the foot of that bed sat the real Peter—singed and alert, ready to speak.
question: When Karen reaches the bed with the fully bandaged patient, what will she do?
answer_with_knowledge: Bypass the bed and turn to the singed man in the wheelchair, embracing him instead.
answer_without_knowledge: Lean over the bandaged patient, and weep into his chest.
